description_long: This shelter is a small, rectangular wooden structure with an exposed frame and a pitched gabled roof. The roof is constructed from evenly spaced wooden rafters and slats, leaving the interior visible from above. The walls are partially enclosed, with visible vertical and horizontal wooden beams forming the main support structure. One side features a framed window and a large rectangular opening that could serve as a doorway. The overall construction suggests an unfinished or under-construction state, typical of a simple utility shed or park shelter. The color palette is dominated by natural wood tones and black wall panels, with some white trim around the window and door frame.
description: A small, unfinished wooden shelter with a gabled roof, exposed framing, and partial wall enclosures. Features a window and a large doorway opening.
description_view_0: Front view shows the gabled roof, framed window, and a large rectangular doorway opening.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the open roof structure, interior framing, and both the window and doorway openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the gabled roof and mostly enclosed back wall with no visible openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the open roof rafters and the interior space, with no window or door on this side.
attribute_roof_type: gabled wooden slat roof with exposed rafters
attribute_entryway_design: rectangular open doorway with simple wooden framing
attribute_support_structure: vertical and horizontal wooden beams with cross-bracing
attribute_transparency: mostly opaque with a single transparent window panel
